SEARCH
Package shot_boxes.questions

Class Questions

java.lang.Object
shot_boxes.questions.Questions
All Implemented Interfaces:
ShotBoxes
Direct Known Subclasses:
EasyQuestions, HardQuestions, MidQuestions
public abstract class Questions extends Object implements ShotBoxes
The Questions abstract class represents question objects that fall during gameplay, and can be interacted with by the player to loss health as penalty.

Subclasses determine the difficulty (Easy, Medium, Hard).

Version:
1.0
Author:

    • Questions

      public Questions()

  • Method Details

    • uploudQuestions

      public static void uploudQuestions(String filePath)
      Loads iquestions from a text file. The file should be structured with difficulty headers ("Easy", "Medium", "Hard") followed by lines of text question.
      Parameters:
      filePath - the path to the file containing the question data

    • draw

      public void draw(Image image, Graphics g)

    • interact

      public void interact(KnowledgeKeepers k)
      Handles the logic when a player interacts with this question.

      Stops the movement, resets the vertical position, and notifies the KnowledgeKeeper that the question has been handled.

      Specified by:
      interact in interface ShotBoxes
      Parameters:
      k - the KnowledgeKeeper who shooted this ShotBox

    • getEasyQuestions

      public static List<Questions> getEasyQuestions()
      Returns the list of easy questions.
      Returns:
      a list containing Questions objects of easy difficulty

    • getMidQuestions

      public static List<Questions> getMidQuestions()
      Returns the list of medium questions.
      Returns:
      a list containing Questions objects of medium difficulty

    • getHardQuestions

      public static List<Questions> getHardQuestions()
      Returns the list of hard questions.
      Returns:
      a list containing Questions objects of hard difficulty

    • getCoordinate

      public int[] getCoordinate()
      Returns the current X and Y coordinates of the question.
      Specified by:
      getCoordinate in interface ShotBoxes
      Returns:
      an array with two elements: X and Y position

    • getQuest

      public String getQuest()
      Returns the question text.
      Returns:
      the question string

    • getPoint

      public int getPoint()
      Returns the point value associated with this question.
      Returns:
      the point value
